title: The Wrestler
logline: An aging professional wrestler past his prime struggles to reconnect with his estranged daughter and find meaning beyond the ring, even as his body fails and his world crumbles around him.
antagonistOrConflict: Time, aging, and Randy's inability to let go of his wrestling persona to embrace real relationships and responsibility

actBreaks.description: Randy suffers a heart attack during a hardcore wrestling match, forcing him to confront his mortality and the unsustainability of his lifestyle.

validation.ruling: Scene #41 shows Randy unconscious in post-op recovery with breathing tubes and mechanical ventilation, ending with 'FADE TO BLACK' - a major irreversible moment that fundamentally changes the story from his wrestling career to his struggle with mortality.

actBreaks.description: Randy violently confronts Stephanie after she rejects him, destroying their fragile reconciliation and pushing him toward his final, fatal choice.

validation.ruling: Scene #107 shows Randy being definitively rejected by his daughter as she tells someone on the phone 'He's gone' and closes the door on him, leaving him crying alone on her doorstep - the final severing of his last meaningful relationship that propels him toward his fatal choice.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a classical three-act tragedy disguised as a character study. The structure follows Randy's journey from denial through forced awakening to ultimate self-destruction. Act I establishes Randy's precarious existence and introduces the key relationships that could save him. The heart attack at the end of Act I serves as both literal and metaphorical wake-up call, forcing Randy into a period of attempted rehabilitation and connection. Act II becomes a sustained attempt at redemption - Randy tries to build a real relationship with his daughter Stephanie and finds genuine connection with Cassidy. However, his inability to truly change, combined with his deep-seated need for the validation that only wrestling provides, undermines these efforts. The structure is relentlessly linear, following Randy's psychological arc without flashbacks or time jumps, which intensifies the sense of inexorable momentum toward his final choice.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately methodical in the first act, using short scenes to establish Randy's routine and the grinding nature of his existence. After the heart attack, the pace slows further as Randy attempts to build relationships, with longer scenes allowing for deeper character development. The final act accelerates dramatically, with rapid scene cuts building to the climactic wrestling match that serves as both literal and metaphorical finale.

incitingIncident.description: Randy meets with Dr. Tarachandani who explains he had a bypass surgery and must stop wrestling, take medication daily, avoid smoking and steroids, but Randy refuses to accept he can't wrestle anymore.

validation.ruling: Scene #41 cannot be the inciting incident as it's already identified as an act break. Scene #43 is the true inciting incident where Dr. Tarachandani tells Randy he had bypass surgery and must stop wrestling forever, shattering Randy's identity and forcing him into the central conflict of choosing between his health and his passion.

midpoint.description: Randy and Cassidy have beers at a bar while he looks at photos of her son on her phone, she reveals she's quitting stripping and moving to a condo near Trenton for better schools, then Randy gets her to dance with him to Def Leppard despite her protests.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. LOCAL BAR - SHORT TIME LATER

validation.ruling: Scene #76 shows Randy and Cassidy bonding over drinks and photos of her son, where she reveals she's quitting stripping and moving away for a better life. This represents Randy's shift from reactive survival to proactively pursuing meaningful relationships, marking his emotional point of no return.

climax.description: Randy performs his entrance ritual, gets bloody from ramming chairs, enters the ring, and thanks the crowd before the match begins with The Ayatollah.

validation.ruling: Scene #137 is just Randy's entrance, not the climax itself. Scene #138 is the actual climax where Randy performs his entrance ritual, gets bloody from ramming chairs, enters the ring, thanks the crowd, and begins his final match with The Ayatollah - the ultimate confrontation where he chooses wrestling over life.

scenes.heading: INT. EAST WILMINGTON HIGH SCHOOL - LOCKER ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Randy sits alone in a high school locker room after a wrestling match, removing his purple spandex tights and revealing his scarred, exhausted body.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST WILMINGTON HIGH SCHOOL - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy limps out of the school and signs autographs for two fans who have old wrestling memorabilia from his WWF days.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy drives his van to Paradise Oaks Trailer Park while listening to Cinderella on the stereo.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARADISE OAKS - RANDY’S TRAILER - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy discovers his trailer door is padlocked and violently kicks the lock in frustration after being unable to open it.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANDY’S TRAILER - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy resignedly climbs into the back of his van to sleep.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK OF VAN - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy lies on a mattress in his van surrounded by a shrine to his wrestling career, taking pills and drinking beer before trying to sleep.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK OF VAN - NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Randy wakes up in his van to kids banging on the outside and calling his name.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARADISE OAKS - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy playfully wrestles with four kids outside his van, performing fake wrestling moves on them until a car arrives at the manager's office.

scenes.heading: INT. MANAGER’S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Randy argues with trailer park manager Len about getting back into his locked trailer, eventually paying twenty dollars to park in back.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARADISE OAKS - IN BACK - EVENING
scenes.summary: Randy sits by his van making a protein shake using an extension cord from a neighbor's trailer while a woman named Meg comes outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHOP-RITE SUPERMARKET - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy enters a Shop-Rite supermarket.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOP-RITE - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sticks his head into the manager's office to speak with Wayne.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy asks store manager Wayne for extra shifts after Wayne makes him knock and re-enter the office properly, enduring Wayne's mocking comments about wrestling.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHOP-RITE - REAR LOADING DOCK - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy works at the Shop-Rite loading dock, carrying boxes from a delivery truck into a walk-in cooler alongside Mexican workers.

scenes.heading: INT. OGDEN’S GYM - LOCKER ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy reluctantly buys Mexican steroids from bodybuilder Gregg in a gym locker room after being unable to get his preferred German version.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM - TOILET STALL - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy injects steroids into his buttocks in a toilet stall.

scenes.heading: INT. GYM FLOOR - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy bench presses weights with a spotter encouraging him to complete another rep.

scenes.heading: INT. SUNSATIONS TANNING SALON - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy enters a tanning salon and greets the front desk woman who directs him to booth four.

scenes.heading: INT. SUNSATIONS TANNING SALON - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy stands naked inside an upright tanning pod under UV lights.

scenes.heading: INT. LOOKING GOOD HAIR & NAILS - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sits in a hair salon chair while a Korean woman bleaches his roots, warning her not to leave the bleach in too long.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy drives along Route 21 listening to Great White, drumming on the dashboard and passing Cheetah's strip club.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy continues driving to AC/DC and pulls into the parking lot of a VFW Hall.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSAIC VFW HALL - CAFETERIA - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Promoter Nick Volpe addresses twenty wrestlers in a VFW cafeteria, announcing the match lineup including Randy's main event against Tommy Rotten.

scenes.heading: INT. CAFETERIA - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sits alone at a table methodically wrapping his knees and ankles with athletic tape in a ritualistic manner.

scenes.heading: INT. CAFETERIA - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy plans his wrestling match with opponent Kid Loco, explaining when Kid Loco should make him bleed, before being interrupted by Lex Lethal about a visitor.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - OUTSIDE CAFETERIA - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Randy's estranged daughter Stephanie arrives to make amends as part of her AA program, telling him she's getting married and doesn't want him there, then leaves after he fails to take responsibility for their relationship.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randy's daughter confronts him about their broken relationship and explicitly excludes him from her wedding, forcing him to face the consequences of his failures as a father.

scenes.heading: INT. THE RING - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy wrestles Kid Loco in the ring, taking a hard lateral drop before secretly cutting himself with a razor blade to bleed for the crowd.

scenes.heading: INT. CAFETERIA - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sits on a high after his match while a medic stitches his head wound, then discusses with promoter Nick Volpe about doing a 20th anniversary rematch with the Ayatollah.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHEETAH’S - PARKING LOT - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy arrives at Cheetah's strip club and greets bouncer Big Chris, who asks him for steroids while letting him in for free.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEETAH’S - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy enters the strip club, orders a beer, and asks the bartender about Cassidy while watching a young stripper perform on stage.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEETAH’S - MEN’S ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Randy uses the men's room and primps in the mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEETAH’S - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy overhears young bachelor party customers rejecting stripper Cassidy for being too old, calling her their mom's age, which angers him as he watches through the VIP room curtain.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEETAH’S - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy gets a lap dance from Cassidy while telling her about his historic wrestling match and upcoming rematch, and she responds positively to his compliments about her appearance.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy drives his van over the Throgs Neck Bridge.

scenes.heading: EXT. RESIDENTIAL STREET - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy's van stops at a house in Queens where Lex Lethal gets out.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy drives Lex Lethal on the Long Island Expressway while they discuss an upcoming hardcore wrestling show and its expected attendance.

scenes.heading: INT. DOLLAR TREE - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy and Lex shop for weapons at a dollar store, testing cake pans and selecting steel wool pads and other items for their hardcore wrestling match.

scenes.heading: INT. NORTH BABYLON COMMUNITY CENTER - GYM - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy makes his entrance at a hardcore wrestling show and begins a brutal match involving staple guns, thumbtacks, and barbed wire before suddenly collapsing in the ring clutching his chest.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randy suffers what appears to be a heart attack during his hardcore wrestling match, marking the moment his body finally gives out from years of abuse.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy is carried down a hallway by wrestlers and fans while Lex tries to keep him conscious.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM/STAGING AREA - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy lies unconscious on a table in the locker room with his eyes rolling back as EMS workers rush in to help him.

scenes.heading: INT. SUFFOLK COUNTY HOSPITAL - POST-OP RECOVERY ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Randy lies unconscious in a hospital recovery room with a breathing tube and chest tube, hooked up to a mechanical ventilator.

scenes.heading: INT. POST-OP RECOVERY ROOM - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Randy wakes up disoriented in the hospital and starts pulling off his medical wires before fainting as nurses rush to reattach them.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S ROOM - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Randy meets with Dr. Tarachandani who explains he had a bypass surgery and must stop wrestling, take medication daily, avoid smoking and steroids, but Randy refuses to accept he can't wrestle anymore.
scenes.turningPointNote: The doctor definitively tells Randy he can never wrestle again, forcing him to confront the end of his wrestling career and identity.

scenes.heading: INT. SUFFOLK COUNTY HOSPITAL - ADMISSIONS DESK - MORNING
scenes.summary: Randy checks out of the hospital and receives a note from Jerry DiFusco with $300 and an offer for another wrestling show, which he finds ironically amusing.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUFFOLK COUNTY HOSPITAL - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy steps out of the hospital into bright daylight, squinting and looking around like a lost animal.

scenes.heading: EXT. NORTH BABYLON COMMUNITY CENTER - PARKING LOT - SHORT
scenes.summary: Randy takes a cab to retrieve his van from the community center parking lot where his wrestling match took place.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sits alone in his van in the empty parking lot, pondering his next move.

scenes.heading: INT. CVS PHARMACY - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy picks up his prescription at CVS pharmacy, embarrassed when the pharmacist calls him by his real name Robin Ramovic.

scenes.heading: INT. PARADISE OAKS - MANAGER’S OFFICE - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy pays his back rent to trailer park manager Len, who notices something seems off about him.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANDY’S TRAILER - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Len removes the padlock from Randy's trailer door and welcomes him home.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY'S TRAILER - KITCHENETTE - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy stands in his messy trailer kitchen examining four prescription pill bottles from CVS, all labeled with the patient name 'RAMOVIC, ROBIN'.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - BATHROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy showers carefully, avoiding getting his surgery bandage on his chest wet.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy collapses exhausted on his bed.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy lies restlessly on his bed and notices an old Nintendo game system on the floor with WrestleJam '88 cartridge.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANDY’S TRAILER - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sticks his head out of his trailer and invites Adam, an 8-year-old neighbor kid, to play Nintendo.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy and Adam play WrestleJam '88 on Nintendo, with Randy getting pissy when Adam criticizes the old graphics and calls 1988 ancient, until Adam's mom calls him home.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy attempts jumping jacks alone in his trailer, gets winded and woozy, sits down to slow his heart, then breaks down crying angry, bitter, frightened tears.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEETAH’S - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Randy enters Cheetah's looking for Cassidy, finds her working, awkwardly asks her to grab a burger and talk somewhere quiet, then reveals he had a heart attack.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHEETAH’S - REAR PARKING LOT - 15 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Randy and Cassidy sit in his van in the club's parking lot as he tells her about his heart attack during wrestling, joking perversely that it was a hit with fans and boosted merchandise sales.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy sits on his bed looking through a junk-filled shoebox and pulls out an old photo of himself with infant daughter Stephanie at Christmas, examining crossed-out phone numbers written on the back.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARADISE OAKS - PAY PHONE - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy calls Stephanie from a pay phone using the photo's phone number, reaches her answering machine, and hangs up without leaving a message.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sits in his van outside a modest house, working up nerve to approach.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEPHANIE’S HOUSE - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy rings the doorbell at Stephanie's house, a woman in underwear answers, Stephanie emerges with a backpack and textbook and tries to blow past him, but he catches up asking to talk.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy drives Stephanie in his van, tells her about his heart attack, and says he wants to try to fix things between them, while she sits in calm silence.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Randy lies depressed on his bed gazing at a day planner open to a page with 'RAHWAY AUTOGRAF SHOW' scribbled in marker.

scenes.heading: INT. RAHWAY RECREATION CENTER - GYMNASIUM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Randy sets up his 8x10 wrestling photos at an autograph show in a gym, where promoter Scott Brumberg asks about his collapse, and Randy coyly claims he sold it well as a work.

scenes.heading: INT. GYMNASIUM - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sits at his autograph table signing a photo for a fan who pays him five dollars, which he tucks into his nearly empty fanny pack.

scenes.heading: INT. GYMNASIUM - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sits bored at his table with no customers, observing other washed-up wrestlers including one asleep, one homeless-looking, and one in a wheelchair with a urine bag, while staring numbly at his old photos.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy pulls up to his trailer, sits scared to go in alone, then pulls back out again.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEETAH’S - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy finds Cassidy at Cheetah's after she's been rejected by multiple customers, they sit and talk about his daughter tearing him a new asshole, and he mentions wanting to get Stephanie a present while Cassidy suggests they go shopping.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOP-RITE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Randy knocks on Wayne's office door at Shop-Rite.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy asks Wayne for steady full-time work, Wayne offers him a deli counter position that includes weekends, and Randy hesitantly accepts despite his wrestling schedule conflict.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randy takes a regular job, signaling his first concrete step away from wrestling

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNRISE SHOPPING PLAZA - PARKING LOT - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy waits outside a vintage clothing store for Cassidy, and when she arrives in street clothes and no makeup, he says he almost didn't recognize her because she looks 'clean'.

scenes.heading: INT. YESTERDAZE - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy and Cassidy shop through boxes of vintage clothes at Yesterdaze, with Randy excited but indecisive about items for his daughter, until they settle on a green satin jacket with an 'S' on it.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUNRISE SHOPPING PLAZA - PARKING LOT - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy thanks Cassidy for helping him shop and suggests getting a beer, she tries to leave saying she has a kid at home, he's surprised to learn she has a 9-year-old son named Daniel, and she reluctantly agrees to one beer.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCAL BAR - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy and Cassidy have beers at a bar while he looks at photos of her son on her phone, she reveals she's quitting stripping and moving to a condo near Trenton for better schools, then Randy gets her to dance with him to Def Leppard despite her protests.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randy and Cassidy's relationship deepens beyond the strip club as they share personal details and dance together

scenes.heading: INT. SHOP-RITE - EMPLOYEE ROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Randy puts on his Shop-Rite deli uniform for his first day, including hairnet and baseball cap, and feels silly looking at himself in the mirror.

scenes.heading: INT. DELI COUNTER - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy works the deli counter serving customers with take-a-number tickets, looking clueless when an elderly woman asks about turkey sale details from a circular.

scenes.heading: INT. DELI COUNTER - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy serves customer number 46, a workout woman ordering pesto pasta salad, and pleases her with his 'pesto change-o' pun and accurate weighing skills.

scenes.heading: INT. DELI COUNTER - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy calls number 57 'bingo' and charms a housewife customer by catching a container behind his back while taking her order for chopped liver and egg salad.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIDDLESEX COUNTY COMMUNITY COLLEGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy surprises Stephanie outside her college with a lumpily wrapped present - the green satin jacket with 'S' for Stephanie - which she finds shiny but clearly wouldn't wear.

scenes.heading: EXT. POINT PLEASANT BOARDWALK - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy and Stephanie walk along the Point Pleasant boardwalk as he tries to jog her memory about childhood visits, pointing out where a funhouse called the Monster Motel used to be.

scenes.heading: EXT. BOARDWALK - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy and Stephanie sit on a bench eating ice cream as Randy tells her he accepts her apology, forgives her for everything, and apologizes for his own failures, surprising her with his sincere emotion.

scenes.heading: INT. BOARDWALK ARCADE - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy and Stephanie play Skee-Ball side-by-side in a boardwalk arcade.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEPHANIE’S HOUSE - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy drops Stephanie off at her house, they shake hands and hug mechanically, he asks about dinner and she initially declines Tuesday but suggests Wednesday would work better.
scenes.turningPointNote: Stephanie agrees to dinner, showing their relationship is genuinely improving

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy puts the old Christmas photo of himself and baby Stephanie on his refrigerator.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARADISE OAKS - PAY PHONE - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy calls someone named Miggy from a pay phone while holding his day planner of upcoming gigs, telling him to count him out for Utica because he's retiring.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randy begins calling promoters to announce his retirement from wrestling

scenes.heading: EXT. PAY PHONE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Randy makes another retirement call to someone named Frank.

scenes.heading: EXT. PAY PHONE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Randy makes another retirement call while holding a Fanfest flyer, telling Volp he's done and retired, positive it's time to move on.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEETAH’S - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Randy watches Cassidy perform on stage at Cheetah's, then approaches with a purple envelope which she takes by hand instead of letting him put it in her G-string like a dollar bill.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEETAH’S - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy gives Cassidy a 'Thanks A Bunch' greeting card and asks her to Mother Kelly's to see a cover band, but she tells him she can't do 'this' between them, explaining she doesn't date customers and is trying to get out of the business.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cassidy firmly rejects Randy's romantic advances, establishing clear boundaries

scenes.heading: INT. SHOP-RITE - DELI COUNTER - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy works the deli counter serving customers including a demanding old lady who makes him repeatedly adjust the weight of her German potato salad, testing his patience as he tries to contain his aggravation.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - LATE NIGHT
scenes.summary: Randy sits shirtless and unable to sleep, watching NBA highlights on TV, touching his chest scar tenderly, finishing a bottle of wine, and cranking up Guns N' Roses music.

scenes.heading: INT. NINTH INNING COLLECTIBLES - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy visits Scott Brumberg at his sports card shop and asks about doing some refereeing work.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIDGEPORT NORTH HIGH SCHOOL - CAFETERIA - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy puts on a referee uniform at a high school cafeteria wrestling event, where Booker D suggests working him into the match, but Randy insists he's not looking to mix it up tonight.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIDGEPORT NORTH H.S. - GYMNASIUM - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy referees a match between Booker D and Shawn McPride, watching the action with tempted, salivating eyes as the crowd goes crazy for the wrestling.

scenes.heading: INT. BOYS’ LOCKER ROOM - SHOWER AREA - POST-MATCH
scenes.summary: Randy showers with Booker D and McPride after the match, with them praising him as 'the master' and noting he got involved despite claiming he was just refereeing.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randy breaks his retirement by getting physically involved in the match, showing he can't stay away from wrestling

scenes.heading: INT. HOLIDAY INN BRIDGEPORT - HOTEL BAR - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy holds court with Booker D and McPride at a hotel bar, all getting drunk as he tells wrestling road stories and they hang on his every word.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL BAR - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy dances with a young woman named Alyssa who recognizes him from her brother's poster and runs her hands up his sides, noticing something about his chest.

scenes.heading: INT. WOMEN’S BATHROOM - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy and Alyssa do bumps of cocaine together in a single-person women's bathroom.

scenes.heading: INT. WOMEN’S BATHROOM - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy has sex with Alyssa in a bathroom, then wakes up naked and disoriented in her bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy, wearing only briefs, encounters Alyssa's roommate in the kitchen who tells him Alyssa is in the shower.

scenes.heading: EXT. APARTMENT BUILDING - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy emerges from Alyssa's apartment building in yesterday's clothes, squinting at the sunlight.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy returns to his trailer and sees the photo of him and Stephanie on the fridge, his expression changing to deep dismay.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEPHANIE’S HOUSE - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy rings Stephanie's doorbell and her friend answers coldly, then closes the door on him while he waits awkwardly outside.

scenes.heading: INT. STEPHANIE’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy enters Stephanie's house uninvited and she confronts him about missing their dinner, calling him a fuck-up and telling him he's dead to her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Stephanie definitively cuts Randy out of her life, severing their relationship completely

scenes.heading: EXT. STEPHANIE’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy steps outside Stephanie's house as she closes the door on him, leaving him alone on her front step where he starts to cry.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOP-RITE - DELI COUNTER - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy works slowly at the deli counter while customers complain, then deliberately moves his thumb closer to the spinning blade of the meat slicer.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sits in his van in the parking lot with dried blood on his face, looking at himself in the mirror and liking what he sees.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARADISE OAKS - PAY PHONE - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy calls Nick from a pay phone asking to get back into wrestling and the match with Bob, saying he just wants to wrestle.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randy decides to return to wrestling despite his heart condition, choosing his wrestling identity over his health

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - DAY
scenes.summary: Randy digs through his closet and pulls out his wrestling tights.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - BATHROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy combs peroxide through his hair while wearing rubber gloves.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - BATHROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy shaves the hair that has grown back on his chest since he last wrestled.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy stands in his trailer wearing only briefs and sprays his body with artificial tanning spray.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Cassidy arrives at Randy's trailer unexpectedly and apologizes for calling him just a customer, explaining she needs to maintain boundaries to live with herself.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDY’S TRAILER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cassidy follows Randy into his trailer explaining she's leaving the stripping world behind and can't bring anyone with her, while Randy dismissively packs his bag.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANDY’S TRAILER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy gets in his van and tells Cassidy he has a wrestling match, handing her the Fanfest flyer before driving away as she looks concerned.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - EVENING
scenes.summary: Randy drives south on the New Jersey Turnpike listening to music.

scenes.heading: INT. CASSIDY’S APARTMENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cassidy sees her son Daniel playing with the Randy The Ram doll, making it perform wrestling moves on other toys.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEETAH’S - LATER
scenes.summary: Cassidy dances onstage at the strip club but appears distracted and abruptly walks off mid-song.

scenes.heading: INT. DRESSING AREA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cassidy empties her locker at the strip club, throwing everything including the lock into her duffel bag.

scenes.heading: INT. CHEETAH’S - MAIN ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cassidy walks across the empty stage with her bag, heading toward the exit as the DJ calls after her.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHEETAH’S - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cassidy steps out of the strip club and says her real name 'Pam' to herself.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cassidy quits stripping and reclaims her real identity as Pam, mirroring Randy's choice to return to his wrestling persona

scenes.heading: EXT. I-95 - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy calls Stephanie's voicemail from a roadside pay phone, promising never to call again and telling her he loves her before hanging up.

scenes.heading: INT. CASSIDY’S CAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cassidy drives south on the turnpike while talking on her phone about leaving food for someone, with the Fanfest flyer on her passenger seat.

scenes.heading: EXT. I-95 - SIDE OF THE ROAD - MIDNIGHT
scenes.summary: Randy's van is pulled over on the side of the interstate at midnight.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK OF VAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy sleeps in the back of his van as trucks barrel past on the highway.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN - NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: Randy pulls into the parking lot of the Greenville Civic Auditorium the next morning.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Randy enters the locker room and encounters The Ayatollah, who is now a successful car dealer, talking about his business expansion.

scenes.heading: INT. GAS STATION - SHORT TIME LATER
scenes.summary: Cassidy stops at a gas station and asks an attendant for directions to the Civic Auditorium.

scenes.heading: INT. GREENVILLE CIVIC AUDITORIUM - LOCKER ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy sits alone in the locker room taping his legs while match sounds come through the wall, his focus and intensity building.

scenes.heading: EXT. GREENVILLE CIVIC AUDITORIUM - PARKING LOT - SHORT TIME
scenes.summary: Cassidy arrives at the auditorium parking lot, which now has many more cars than when Randy arrived.

scenes.heading: INT. GREENVILLE CIVIC AUDITORIUM - OUTER CONCOURSE - SHORT
scenes.summary: Cassidy enters the auditorium building and looks around the concourse, unsure where to go.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Randy paces in his wrestling tights as Arabic music plays and The Ayatollah prepares for their entrance with an Iranian flag.

scenes.heading: INT. OUTER CONCOURSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cassidy walks around the concourse with mounting urgency and approaches a security guard by an unmarked door.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cassidy finds Randy in the locker room and begs him not to wrestle, revealing she quit her job for him, but he dismisses the real world and chooses wrestling.
scenes.turningPointNote: Randy definitively chooses his wrestling persona over real relationships and his health, rejecting Cassidy's love and sacrifice

scenes.heading: INT. AUDITORIUM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy bursts through the curtain into the auditorium to huge cheers from the 700-person crowd.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMENTATORS’ TABLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randy performs his entrance ritual, gets bloody from ramming chairs, enters the ring, and thanks the crowd before the match begins with The Ayatollah.

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
